page.title: RBAC Overview
page.helpText: Browse RBAC bindings across the cluster. Each tab answers a different question — elevated admins, access in a namespace, permissions for one subject, or who can perform an action. Filter tables and click names to open resources.
page.noTabAccess: You do not have permission to view any RBAC Overview tabs. Ask a cluster administrator for list or access-review permissions in your namespace.
tabIntro.whoCan: Who can lists users and groups allowed to perform an action. Can subject checks whether one user, group, or service account is allowed. Cluster-scoped resources (for example nodes) do not need a namespace.
tabIntro.clusterAdmins: Find subjects with elevated or sensitive roles. Names come from plugin-config.json and ClusterRoles labeled {{labelKey}}={{labelValue}}.
tabIntro.namespaceAccess: Choose a namespace to see who has access there. In namespace lists local RoleBindings; Cluster-wide lists ClusterRoleBindings that apply to every namespace, including the one you selected.
tabIntro.subjects: Pick a user, group, or service account to see which roles are bound to it. The list includes every subject referenced in RoleBindings and ClusterRoleBindings you can list, plus OpenShift User, Group, and ServiceAccount objects when list permission is granted. System groups and users without a User API object still appear when they are named in a binding.
tabIntro.scc: Lists direct SCC authorizations and ClusterRoleBindings to ClusterRoles that grant use on securitycontextconstraints.
tabIntro.roleAccess: Pick a ClusterRole to see ClusterRoleBindings and RoleBindings that reference it. Expand role rules inline where supported.
column.name: Name
column.subject: Subject
empty.noFilterMatches: No rows match the current filters.
whoCan.noSubjects: No subjects returned for this query.
whoCan.errorSelectNamespace: Select a namespace. The console "All projects" view cannot be used — who-can is scoped to one namespace (for example default).
whoCan.errorClusterPermission: You do not have permission to run cluster-scoped who-can queries. This requires create on resourceaccessreviews (typically cluster-admin).
whoCan.errorNamespacePermission: You do not have permission to run who-can in namespace "{{namespace}}". This requires create on localresourceaccessreviews (typically cluster-admin).
canSubject.noReason: No reason returned by the API.
canSubject.errorSelectNamespace: Select a namespace. The console "All projects" view cannot be used — can-subject is scoped to one namespace (for example default).
canSubject.errorClusterPermission: You do not have permission to run can-subject queries. This requires create on subjectaccessreviews (typically cluster-admin).
canSubject.errorNamespacePermission: You do not have permission to run can-subject in namespace "{{namespace}}". This requires create on localsubjectaccessreviews (typically cluster-admin).
canSubject.userChecksIncludeGroups: User checks include OpenShift group memberships in the access review:
canSubject.noGroupsForUser: No group memberships were found for this user. Only direct user permissions are evaluated.
myAccess.title: My access
myAccess.noGroupMemberships: No OpenShift group memberships found for your user.
myAccess.noVisibleProjects: No projects are visible with your current permissions.
myAccess.clusterBindingsHidden: ClusterRoleBindings are hidden because you cannot list cluster role bindings. Namespace RoleBindings you can access are shown below.
myAccess.noLoggedInUser: Could not determine the logged-in user.
clusterAdmins.clusterWideSectionBody: Cluster-scoped bindings to sensitive roles (from plugin-config.json and elevated ClusterRole labels). Use resource links to open subjects, bindings, or roles.
clusterAdmins.namespaceElevatedSectionBo: Admin rights limited to the listed namespace. These bindings do not grant cluster-wide access. Click a namespace to open its project.
namespaceAccess.selectPrompt: Select a namespace to list subjects with access.
namespaceAccess.namespaceSubjectCount: {{count}} unique subject(s) from RoleBindings in this namespace.
namespaceAccess.noNamespaceRoleBindings: No RoleBindings found in this namespace.
namespaceAccess.clusterWideSubjectCount: {{count}} unique subject(s) with ClusterRoleBindings that apply to all namespaces, including {{namespace}}.
subjects.notFoundBody: The subject from the URL was not found in the list you can access.
subjects.noFilterMatches: No subjects match the current filter.
subjects.noSubjectsVisible: No subjects are visible with your current permissions. You need list access to RoleBindings or ClusterRoleBindings, or to User, Group, or ServiceAccount resources.
subjects.listCountDetail: {{total}} subjects ({{bindings}} from bindings, {{api}} from OpenShift resources).
subjects.selectPrompt: Choose a user, group, or service account to inspect its role bindings.
subjects.noRoleBindings: No role bindings found for this subject.
scc.title: Security context constraints
scc.summary: {{count}} security context constraint(s) in the cluster.
scc.rbacSectionBody: Subjects listed here receive SCC use permission via ClusterRoleBindings to ClusterRoles whose rules include use on securitycontextconstraints.
scc.noRbacGrants: No RBAC-derived SCC use grants found.
roleRules.onlyClusterOrRole: Rules can only be loaded for ClusterRole or namespaced Role resources.
roleRules.none: No policy rules found for this role.
roleRules.aggregatedBody: This ClusterRole uses aggregationRule. Rules shown here are only the direct rules on this object; effective permissions may include additional rules from aggregated ClusterRoles.
identity.serviceAccountBody: Service account {{namespace}}/{{name}} is a Kubernetes identity in namespace {{namespace}}.
identity.noIdpMappings: No identity provider mappings found for this user.
identity.noGroupMembers: No users are listed as members of this group.
permission.limitedListCrb: You do not have permission to list cluster role bindings. Some tabs may show incomplete data.
permission.limitedTab: You cannot {{permissions}}. This tab may show incomplete or empty data.
permission.tabDeniedWithList: You do not have permission to use this tab. You cannot {{permissions}}.
permission.tabDenied: You do not have permission to use this tab.
permission.selfSubjectReview: You do not have permission to review your own subject (create selfsubjectreviews).
